data.body:
## Context
Practitioners developing DSO plugin skills, agents, and prompt templates frequently reference scripts using direct paths (plugins/dso/scripts/, $PLUGIN_SCRIPTS/, ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/) instead of the portable .claude/scripts/dso shim. When these instruction files are consumed by agents executing in host project contexts, the direct paths don't resolve — the plugin is installed at a different location than plugins/dso/. This causes agent execution failures that are difficult to diagnose because the agent sees a "command not found" error with no indication that the shim should have been used instead. Today there are 152 such violations across 48 files in plugins/dso/, meaning every host project consuming this plugin is exposed to these failures whenever an agent follows a skill or workflow instruction containing a direct path.

## Success Criteria
1. A check-shim-refs.sh script detects direct script path references in instruction files within plugins/dso/ — accepts file path args for targeted scans, defaults to full in-scope set (skills, agents, prompts, workflows, living docs — excluding scripts, tests, ADRs, archived docs)
2. check-shim-refs.sh correctly distinguishes executable references (should use shim) from source commands targeting hooks/lib/ (should not) and script-to-script references within plugins/dso/scripts/ (out of scope)
3. check-shim-refs.sh is wired into validate.sh --ci so CI catches violations
4. A pre-commit hook check runs check-shim-refs.sh on staged files within plugins/dso/ to prevent new violations
5. All existing violations in instruction files within plugins/dso/ are resolved — each converted to shim or annotated with exemption comment
6. An integration test verifies (a) check-shim-refs.sh exits non-zero on a file with a direct path reference, and (b) exits zero on a file using the shim

## Scenario Analysis
- Exemption comment syntax mismatch (high): if pattern match is case/whitespace-strict, variants silently bypass detection
- Hook/lib source-command exclusion too broad (high): substring match on plugins/dso/ instead of plugins/dso/hooks/lib/ silently exempts violations
- Script-to-script exclusion bleeds into instruction files (high): exclusion must check containing file path, not referenced target path
- Variable references missed (high): must detect $PLUGIN_SCRIPTS/, ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/ not just literal plugins/dso/scripts/
- validate.sh exit code mismatch (critical): must align exit codes with check-skill-refs.sh convention
- Bulk remediation converts intentionally direct refs (medium): bootstrap/install docs use direct paths by necessity
- check-shim-refs.sh not executable (high): chmod +x in same commit
- Scope divergence between standalone and CI (medium): validate.sh should call without args
- ADR/archive exclusion relies on path convention (low): exclude docs/adr/ and docs/archive/

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Create check-shim-refs.sh following the check-skill-refs.sh pattern — same argument handling, same exit code conventions, same integration points. Wire into validate.sh and the pre-commit hook. Then use the detection output to guide batch remediation of existing violations across file groups (skills, agents, prompts/workflows, living docs), with each violation individually evaluated for correctness before replacement.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 9
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
